ksa_text: Skill in conducting AI security assessments, including threat modeling for AI systems, attack surface analysis, and control gap identification.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: Included: skill maps directly to AI Security Specialist scope — RSK-specific signals detected ("threat model", "attack surface"). STRM composite is strong (0.5760); note that framework scores routinely underweight risk management and governance vocabulary, so text-based signal carries primary weight here. Seniority is appropriate: the KSA describes practice-level execution, not enterprise strategy.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: External check: no matches in RSK job postings or cert curricula (CRISC, FRM, NIST AI RMF, CISM). Would a working AI Security Specialist self-report this as required?
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: Challenge answered: the competency is implicit in AI Security Specialist work (domain affinity 0.85, 3 RSK signal(s)). Framework vocabulary matching systematically underrepresents risk management and governance KSAs.
